output:
CVE-2023-37575 is a high-severity vulnerability involving multiple use-after-free issues within the VCD get_vartoken realloc functionality of GTKWave version 3.3.115. This vulnerability can be exploited through specially crafted .vcd files, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. A successful attack requires the victim to open a malicious file, which interacts with the GUI's interactive VCD parsing code. The vulnerability is classified under CWE-416, which highlights the risks of referencing memory after it has been freed, potentially causing crashes or unauthorized code execution.

The known affected software is GTKWave version 3.3.115. To mitigate against possible exploitation, consider applying recommended cybersecurity practices referenced by MITRE ATT&CK techniques, specifically T1203, which details common malware execution vectors. Mitigation strategies include adopting mitigations M1050, M1051, and M1048, focusing on improving security hygiene and limiting exposure to crafted malicious files in operational environments.
